summary: First E2E smoke test of ClientDesk AI. Backend (8/8 critical endpoints) fully working including Claude Sonnet 4.6 chat, leads, appointments, JWT-protected admin routes. Frontend landing, services grid (14 cards), service detail page, contact form, and full admin auth flow (wrong + correct creds -> dashboard with stats and leads table) all verified. One blocker: floating chatbot button is positioned at bottom-right (fixed bottom-6 right-6) and is overlapped by the 'Made with Emergent' badge which intercepts pointer events. The chatbot widget UI is therefore not openable by a regular user click. Backend /api/chat itself works and returns coherent Claude replies.
passed: POST /api/chat -> 200 returns coherent Claude Sonnet 4.6 reply
POST /api/admin/login correct creds -> 200 returns JWT token
Landing page loads with hero title, primary CTA, 14 service cards
Contact form submission (UI) creates lead and shows success state (visible in admin leads table)
Service detail /service/ai-receptionist loads with title + embedded contact form
Admin /admin/login: correct creds redirects to /admin and renders admin-dashboard + leads-table with rows and stats counters

failed.issue: The floating chatbot open button (data-testid='chatbot-open-btn') is positioned at fixed bottom-6 right-6 with z-50, but the Emergent 'Made with Emergent' badge (id='emergent-badge') is overlaid at the same bottom-right corner and intercepts pointer events. Playwright reports the badge intercepting clicks; even with force=True the chat panel input never becomes visible. End users will not be able to open the chatbot at desktop viewport.
failed.evidence: Playwright error: '<a id="emergent-badge"> intercepts pointer events'. Screenshot shows chatbot button visually behind the badge. Note backend POST /api/chat is fully functional (verified via curl, returns Claude reply).

action_items: Reposition or restyle the chatbot floating button so it is not overlapped by the Emergent badge (e.g., move chatbot-open-btn to bottom-24 right-6, or shift the badge, or raise the chat button z-index above the badge while keeping it accessible). Re-test that clicking the button opens the panel and that sending a message renders the assistant reply in the UI.
